title: Day 7: Recipe Tree
prompt:
Each non-empty line describes one recipe in this exact shape:

`RESULT requires N1 INGRED_A, N2 INGRED_B, ... .`

or

`RESULT requires nothing.`

Result and ingredient names are single lowercase words (no spaces). Counts are positive integers. Return the number of distinct recipes (results) that transitively depend on `binding_paste` — that is, recipes that directly list `binding_paste` as an ingredient OR list any recipe that does. The starting `binding_paste` recipe is not counted as depending on itself.

tests.name: two dependents
args:
core requires 1 binding_paste.
shell requires 1 core.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: no dependents
args:
alpha requires 1 beta.
beta requires nothing.

tests.name: diamond dependents
args:
left requires 1 binding_paste.
right requires 1 binding_paste.
top requires 1 left, 1 right.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: long chain
args:
a requires 1 binding_paste.
b requires 1 a.
c requires 1 b.
d requires 1 c.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: irrelevant subtree
args:
core requires 1 binding_paste.
irrelevant requires 1 dust.
dust requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: binding_paste not referenced anywhere
args:
alpha requires 1 beta.
beta requires 1 gamma.
gamma requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: multiple direct dependents
args:
alpha requires 1 binding_paste.
beta requires 1 binding_paste.
gamma requires 1 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: deep transitive chain
args:
a requires 1 b.
b requires 1 c.
c requires 1 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: mixed ingredient counts dont matter for reachability
args:
tonic requires 5 binding_paste, 3 herb.
herb requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.

parts.title: Part 2: Paste cost
parts.prompt: Same input. Now compute the total quantity of `binding_paste` required to produce exactly one unit of the recipe named `final_product`. `binding_paste` is a raw material: one unit of it counts as 1, and you do not descend into any rule that defines `binding_paste` itself. Direct dependencies multiply through the chain (e.g., 2 of X, and X needs 3 binding_paste each, contributes 6). If `final_product` is not defined in the input, return 0. If `final_product` does not transitively need `binding_paste`, return 0.

tests.name: direct cost
args:
final_product requires 3 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: indirect cost
args:
final_product requires 2 core.
core requires 5 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: no paste needed
args:
final_product requires 1 dust.
dust requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: diamond multiplies twice
args:
final_product requires 2 left, 3 right.
left requires 1 binding_paste.
right requires 4 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: long chain
args:
final_product requires 2 a.
a requires 3 b.
b requires 5 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: diamond with shared deeper subtree
args:
final_product requires 2 a, 2 b.
a requires 3 base.
b requires 3 base.
base requires 2 binding_paste.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: mixed paste and non-paste ingredients
args:
final_product requires 2 binding_paste, 1 sleeve.
sleeve requires 3 binding_paste, 4 leather.
leather requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.

tests.name: final_product directly depends on non-paste leaf
args:
final_product requires 10 sand.
sand requires nothing.
binding_paste requires nothing.
